level_signals: 5+ years in product management on platform / API, integrations, or developer / ecosystem products
Track record shipping integration, connector, or platform products with real impact
Experience working directly with high-stakes customers and ecosystem partners

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years in product management on platform / API, integrations, or developer / ecosystem products.
Track record shipping integration, connector, or platform products with real impact on adoption, expansion, or retention.
Experience working directly with high-stakes customers and ecosystem partners.
Technical fluency with APIs, auth, data models, and integration architecture; comfortable with first-party vs. partner vs. self-serve tradeoffs.
Analytical and metrics-first, and a strong cross-functional collaborator across engineering, design, and field / deployment teams.
Experience with a least a few domains of: Agentic AI, LLMs, Automation, Productivity, Enterprise security and compliance.

scope.surface: North integrations platform — connector framework, auth and identity, tool-use, data sync, permissioning; developer and partner experience (APIs, SDKs, plugin model, docs)
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS, security-first AI platform
core_responsibilities: Define the integration ecosystem strategy for North - roadmap, catalogue priorities, and partner posture - anchored to customer demand and revenue.
Own North's integrations end to end, and partner with strategic customers to define transformative, integrations-based workflows.

Derive generic, platform-level capabilities (connector framework, auth and identity, tool-use, data sync, permissioning) where they don't yet exist, partnering with the core-experience team on shared work.
Collaborate with forward-deployed engineering to define how to evolve integration work into reusable product, to scope, sequence, and build it.
Define the developer and partner experience (APIs, SDKs, plugin model, docs) and instrument analytics to track adoption, time-to-stand-up, and retention.

green_flags: Clear dual mandate articulated: own integrations AND build platform capabilities
Explicit outcome metrics defined (adoption, retention, time-to-stand-up)
Remote-friendly with global offices — broad location flexibility
Generous benefits: 6 weeks PTO, 100% parental leave top-up, robust stipends
JD encourages applicants who don't exactly match all requirements
Well-scoped product surface (North integrations) with clear ownership
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Location type is ambiguous — JD says 'remote-friendly' but does not clarify if this role has any office requirement
Disclosure that AI tools may be used in screening applicants

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level inferred from 5+ years requirement and scope of responsibilities
seniority.manages_people inferred as false — no people management language present
scope.primary_metrics_inferred — inferred from responsibilities and outcomes sections
location.remote_us_eligible inferred as true from 'remote-friendly' language
compensation.equity_mentioned inferred as false — not mentioned in JD
ambiguities: Location type: JD states 'Cohere is remote-friendly' without specifying if this role requires any office presence or is fully remote. Treated as remote-global.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship: not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Marked as unclear.
All eight requirements appear under a 'Requirements' heading with no 'Preferred' or 'Nice to have' subsection, so all are classified as hard must-haves.
